column3: Plaza full - a plaza completely full of a massive overabundance of items.
Ply - like plywood, it bends easily and is highly flexible.
Point - a sad memory so sharp it feels like it points straight into your heart.
Polemic = debate weaponized: a blistering attack essay or speech against a person or idea.
Important - a highly important sign that something terrible is about to happen.
Pot - water safe enough to boil in a pot and drink.
potent (powerful) = an incredibly powerful ruler or dictator.
Press of ice - standing dangerously on the very edge of a massive cliff of ice.
pre- (before) + "cludere" (shut) = shut the door before it happens, making it impossible.
Latin praecox ("cooked before"): a child whose mind has "cooked" ahead of schedule.
Pre-direction - you lean in a pre-direction because you have a strong preference for it.
pre- (before) + "ponder" (weigh) = an amount so massive it outweighs everything else.
Possess - someone so attractive you want to possess their attention.
pre- (before) + "sage" (wise) = wise intuition that reads what is coming before it arrives.
pre- (before) + "sci" (know) = knowing what will happen before it does.
prim (first) + "ev" (age) = relating to the very first, ancient ages of the earth.
Deprive - a state of extreme poverty where you are deprived of basic needs.
Probe - a politician whose morals are so flawless they withstand the deepest legal probe.
Incline-built - in lean toward a habit; a tendency so steady it feels like gravity.
pro- (outside) + "fanum" (temple) = disrespect spoken outside the sacred space.
Profits gate - throwing profits out the gate because you are reckless with money.
pro- (forth) + "fundere" (pour) = pouring forth in massive abundant quantities.
Prom gate - announcing new rules loudly right at the prom gate.
Pro pen - a writer with a natural tendency to always grab a pro pen.
Profits - conditions look favorable; strong signs you could see big profits.
Proper - acting with strict, perfectly proper, socially acceptable behavior.
Prose - writing that sounds like ordinary prose instead of poetry; dull.
pro- (before) + "scribere" (write) = write a name on a list to legally forbid someone.
From Proteus, Greek sea god who changed shape to escape - versatile, constantly shifting.
Prude - so careful and cautious with choices that people joke you are a prude.
Pure ill - a child acting so immaturely it makes you feel purely ill.
pugn (fight) = eager to argue or fight; combative energy.
Pull attitude - beauty so strong it pulls the room's attitude toward you.
Punctuation - obsessed with tiny details; corrects your text-message punctuation.
Punch in - a smell so sharp it punches you in the nose.
pun (punish) = intended to punish, not merely to correct.
Puke - rot so foul it makes you want to puke.
Quack mire - a duck stuck in a muddy, complicated swamp.
Wandering - wandering lost because you are stuck in painful uncertainty.
Quiet well - force quiets the riot well.
Quarrel - whining and always ready to pick a petty quarrel.
Don Quixote charged windmills as giants - idealistic but impractical.
Quota - same boring daily quota; ordinary everyday life.
Derail - ranting so hard you try to derail the meeting.
Rank core - bitter hatred sitting in the core of your heart.
Ruckus - a loud chaotic crowd making a ruckus.
Razor - bulldozing a building down as if shaved by a razor.
re- (back) + "calcitrare" (kick) = kicking back; stubbornly disobedient.
re- (again) + "capit" (head) = revisit the main heads or points in summary.
re- (back) + "clus" (shut) = shut away from the world; withdrawn.
rect (straight/right) = upright, morally straight behavior.
Doubt - an opponent so intimidating you doubt you can win.
Relocate - moved down to a lower, less important rank or role.
Hot dog relish - savoring a moment the way you savor relish on a hot dog.
Miss - careless with duty so you miss the deadline entirely.
re- (back) + "nuntiare" (announce) = announce you formally renounce a claim.
re- (again) + "plere" (fill) = filled back up to maximum capacity.
Pose - lying still in a calm resting pose.
Apprehend - behavior so bad the police should apprehend you.
Roach - scolding the owner for a roach in the kitchen.
Probation - a scoundrel who failed probation and slid back to trouble.
Prove - scolding someone until they prove they will not repeat it.
Refuse it - reject a claim and refuse it as false.
re- (back) + "puls" (drive) = drive an attack or feeling back.
Send back - cancel an order and send it entirely back.
re- (back) + "salire" (jump) = bounce back after a hard hit.
Resolution - a firm resolution you refuse to break.
Rest bit - a badly needed rest bit during an exhausting hike.
Splendid - shining so bright it looks splendid.
Restore - paying or working to restore what was stolen or broken.
Restless - a horse tense and restless, unable to stand still.
re- (back) + "tract" (pull) = formally pull back a false statement.
Rebel - the rebel parties all night after a wild win.
Reverend - deep respectful awe, like toward a reverend.
re- (back) + "voc" (call) = call a license back and cancel it.
Rap song - praising or ranting with the heat of a passionate rap.
Rip bawd - coarse, lewd humor ripped from the tavern; indecent and deliberately shocking.
Rifle - an area rife with rifles; common and dangerous.
Latin ruminare (chew the cud) - chew the same thought over and over.
Confuse - a trick meant to confuse the enemy.
Saccharin - sweetness as fake as the sweetener; cloying and artificial.
sacro (sacred) + "sanct" (holy) = doubly sacred; not to be violated.
Sage - the deep judgment of a wise sage.
Sail - the main sail is the most salient feature on the boat.
Solve - ointment that soothes a burn and solves the worst of the sting.
sanct (holy) = acting holier-than - thou to claim moral high ground.
Latin sanguis (blood): old humor theory tied blood tone to confident optimism.
Satisfy ate - you ate enough to satisfy brutal hunger.
Scalding - a review that scalds; harshly critical.
Sparkling - wit and energy sparkling like champagne bubbles.
Scrap - checking every scrap because honesty demands perfect detail.
Scurry - ugly rumors that make the target want to scurry away.
sed (sit) = life spent sitting; little movement.
semin (seed) = a seed idea that spawns a whole field of later work.
Walpole coined it from "The Three Princes of Serendip" - lucky finds by accident.
serv (serve/slave) = groveling, excessively submissive.
Sinus - a road winding like sinus passages; serpentine curves.
Sole - hovering over whether your shoe sole is hurting your foot.
solus (alone) + "ipse" (self) = the world is only "me" in your mind.
Solve rent - cash flow strong enough to solve rent and pay debts.
somn (sleep) = drowsy, ready to nod off.
Sophomore - acting like a cocky, immature sophomore.
Furious - the judge furious at evidence that is clearly fake.
Stayed - so staid they stayed home; serious, steady, unflashy.
From the Stoa Poikile porch where Zeno taught - endure pain without theatrics.
Solid - emotionally solid like a brick wall; unmovable.
Trident - noise as harsh as a trident slammed into stone.
Stupid - shocked stupid; brain freezes for a moment.
sub (under) + "jugum" (yoke) = bring a people under your yoke.
Sucked in - speech with extra words sucked in; tight and brief.
super (over) + "flu" (flow) = extra detail flowing over the brim.
Surf it - so many waves you cannot surf them all; excess.
Summarize - guess the whole plot from scraps, like forced summarizing.
Syrup - pouring extra syrup when nobody is watching.
Weather-line - outdoor life etched into complexion (literary "swarth" tones).
Psyche + fawn - flatters power to win favor; a professional bootlicker.
Take it - a silent deal where everyone takes it and says nothing.
tacit (silent) = habitually quiet; not chatty.
Tangent - barely grazing the topic; flies off on a tangent.
Total amount - equivalent in seriousness or value to another total.
Team error - reckless nerve to blame the whole team error on the boss.
tenere (hold) = an argument you can hold up under scrutiny.
Ten percent grip - holding the rope by the thinnest tenth; shaky.
Tremble - timid and trembling like a frightened mouse.
Tired - a long angry speech that leaves everyone tired.
Toad - creeping praise like a toad at the tyrant's foot.
Tomb - a huge tome as heavy as a tombstone.
Torpedo - floating sluggish like a torpedo before launch.
Tear it - passion so hot it tears at restraint; scorching heat.
Torture - path so twisting it feels like torture to navigate.
tract (lead/manage) = easy to lead; docile and manageable.
trans (across) + "gress" (step) = step across a moral line you should not.
trans (across) + "ire" (go) = passing through; not staying long.
trans (across) + "mut" (change) = change something into another substance.
Tragedy - a mockery of justice so bad it is a tragedy.
Tremble - voice trembling from fear.
Trench - criticism that cuts a deep trench.
Trap - dread that you are walking into a trap.
Tripe - an idea as tired as cheap tripe.
Truck - aggressive like a truck ready to run you down.
Trunk - chop at the trunk to shorten the tree.
Turgid = swollen - bloated language or tissue, overstuffed and heavy (not muddy/turbid).
Moral filth - conduct courts label crimes of moral turpitude; deep baseness.
ubique (everywhere) = showing up everywhere at once.
umbr (shadow) = take offense as if a disrespectful shadow fell on you.
Oil-on-tone - flattery so smooth it feels greasy; slick, insincere charm.
Undulate - waves moving under the surface in smooth rolls.
Braid - scolding someone for ruining your careful braid.
Use up - seize power to use up what is left of the kingdom.
More coined "Utopia" from ou + topos ("no place") - a perfect society that cannot exist.
Vase - wavering forever between two vases you cannot choose.
vac (empty) = empty behind the eyes; mindless.
Vapor - remark so dull it evaporates like vapor.
varius (various) = patched with varied colors or tones.
Vehicle - yelling with full heat at the car that cut you off.
venerari (respect) = worthy of reverence for age, dignity, or achievement.
Revere-plus - honor deeply as if before something sacred (same family as reverent).
ver (truth) = truthfulness you can stake a claim on.
verb (word) = padded with too many words; talky and windy.
virid (green) = green with lush growth.
Vest - only a ripped old vest remains as a trace of the wreck.
Hex - annoy someone as if you cast a petty hex on them.
Borrowed thrill - living someone else's excitement secondhand.
vilis (vile) + "-fy" (make) = talk someone into looking vile.
Vindicare - show the accusation was false; clear a ruined name.
Victim - wanting the other side to become your suffering victim.
Viscosity - thick, sticky, slow to pour.
Vitriol - words as burning as strong acid.
Viper - hissing insults with viper venom.
viv (life) = lively, bubbling with life.
voc (call) = a career that feels like a calling.
voc (voice) + "ferre" (carry) = carrying a loud voice into the room.
Wayne - Wayne Enterprises lights dimming as Bruce fades after a brutal night.
Wonton - wanton waste of a whole bowl of wonton soup on purpose; reckless malice.
Wile E. - cunning traps and scams like the cartoon coyote.
Win some - charm that wins some hearts instantly.
Wishful - nostalgia colored by sad wishful longing.
Wizard - face wizened like a storybook wizard: shriveled and lined.
Jealous - fire of a jealous lover turned toward a cause; fiercely devoted.
Zenith tip - apex of the arc (sun at noon); your highest point, not the bottom below.
Zero fear - breeze so gentle it gives you zero fear of a storm.
